story: Years after my husband left, I spotted Joe, my first love, in a department-store aisle. We traded smiles. “Hi, Joe.” He nodded at the boy with him. “My nephew. I’ve got to get him to afternoon kindergarten.” We compared kid counts; he said four, then, “Maybe we’ll run into each other again.” I hesitated, unsure if he was brushing me off; I figured he must be married, so I kept it light. Joe had been divorced for a year; seeing me, he almost asked to grab coffee and swap numbers, but he was rushing to drop off his nephew and decided not to. He turned to go.
implausible_knowledge: Joe had been divorced for a year; seeing me, he almost asked to grab coffee and swap numbers, but he was rushing to drop off his nephew and decided not to.
question: As Joe turns to leave after saying he needs to get his nephew to kindergarten, what is Mary most likely to do?
answer_with_knowledge: Stop him to suggest exchanging numbers and meeting for coffee.
answer_without_knowledge: Let him go with a polite goodbye and continue shopping.
